title: [InstCombine] Modify `foldSelectICmpEq` to only handle more useful and simple cases.

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64-p1:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64"

define i32 @src_and_eq_0_add_or(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
entry:
  %and = and i32 %y, %x
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %and, 0
  %add = add i32 %y, %x
  %or = or i32 %y, %x
  %cond = select i1 %cmp, i32 %add, i32 %or
  ret i32 %cond
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions arithmetic operations support but existing tests only cover bitwise ops (and, or, xor). This test uses add in combination with or, which is covered by the patch's MatchAddOrXor matcher but not explicitly demonstrated in the summary examples.

alive2.src:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64-p1:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64"

define i32 @src_and_eq_0_add_or(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
entry:
  %and = and i32 %y, %x
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %and, 0
  %add = add i32 %y, %x
  %or = or i32 %y, %x
  %cond = select i1 %cmp, i32 %add, i32 %or
  ret i32 %cond
}
alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64-p1:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64"

define i32 @src_and_eq_0_add_or(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
entry:
  %or = or i32 %y, %x
  ret i32 %or
}
alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i32 @src_and_eq_0_add_or(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
entry:
  %and = and i32 %y, %x
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %and, 0
  %add = add i32 %y, %x
  %or = or i32 %y, %x
  %cond = select i1 %cmp, i32 %add, i32 %or
  ret i32 %cond
}
=>
define i32 @src_and_eq_0_add_or(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
entry:
  %or = or i32 %y, %x
  ret i32 %or
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64-p1:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64"

define i32 @src_or_eq_0_mul_sub(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
entry:
  %or = or i32 %y, %x
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %or, 0
  %mul = mul i32 %y, %x
  %sub = sub i32 %y, %x
  %cond = select i1 %cmp, i32 %mul, i32 %sub
  ret i32 %cond
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions 'arithmetic operations in the zero-case scenario' but only discusses the (X & Y) == 0 case. The patch actually supports Mul, Sub, and other arithmetic ops for (X | Y) == 0 via IsOpcZeroWithZeros, which is not explicitly covered in the summary text.

alive2.src:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64-p1:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64"

define i32 @src_or_eq_0_mul_sub(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
entry:
  %or = or i32 %y, %x
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %or, 0
  %mul = mul i32 %y, %x
  %sub = sub i32 %y, %x
  %cond = select i1 %cmp, i32 %mul, i32 %sub
  ret i32 %cond
}

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64-p1:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64"

define i32 @src_or_eq_0_zero_binop(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
entry:
  %or = or i32 %y, %x
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %or, 0
  %and = and i32 %y, %x
  %cond = select i1 %cmp, i32 0, i32 %and
  ret i32 %cond
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: The patch explicitly allows the true arm to be just '0' (via match(TrueVal, m_Zero())), but the summary only mentions binary operations in both arms. This case where true arm is a constant zero is not described in the summary.
